lines.english: Nanak grasps the feet of those humble beings. ||3||
In the remembrance of God, many are saved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਾ ਸਿਮਰਨੁ ਸਭ ਤੇ ਊਚਾ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kaa simaran sabh te aoochaa |
lines.english: The remembrance of God is the highest and most exalted of all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਸੁਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kai simaran sabh kichh sujhai |
lines.english: In the remembrance of God, all things are known.
In the remembrance of God, thirst is quenched.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਜਮ ਤ੍ਰਾਸਾ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kai simaran naahee jam traasaa |
lines.english: In the remembrance of God, there is no fear of death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਮਲੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kai simaran man kee mal jaae |
lines.english: In the remembrance of God, the filth of the mind is removed.
In the remembrance of God, hopes are fulfilled.
The Ambrosial Naam, the Name of the Lord, is absorbed into the heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਜੀ ਬਸਹਿ ਸਾਧ ਕੀ ਰਸਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: prabh jee baseh saadh kee rasanaa |
lines.english: God abides upon the tongues of His Saints.
Those who remember God are wealthy.
Nanak is the servant of the slave of His slaves. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਸੇ ਜਨ ਪਰਵਾਨ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh se jan paravaan |
lines.english: Those who remember God are approved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਸੇ ਪੁਰਖ ਪ੍ਰਧਾਨ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh se purakh pradhaan |
lines.english: Those who remember God are the most distinguished persons.
Those who remember God are honorable.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਸਿ ਸਰਬ ਕੇ ਰਾਜੇ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh si sarab ke raaje |
lines.english: Those who remember God are the rulers of all.
Those who remember God dwell in peace.
Those who remember God are not lacking.
Those who remember God are immortal and eternal.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਮਰਨ ਤੇ ਲਾਗੇ ਜਿਨ ਆਪਿ ਦਇਆਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: simaran te laage jin aap deaalaa |
lines.english: They alone hold to the remembrance of Him, unto whom He Himself shows His Mercy.
Those who remember God generously help others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਤਿਨ ਸਦ ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh tin sad balihaaree |
lines.english: Those who remember God - to them, I am forever a sacrifice.
Nanak begs for the dust of their feet. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਤਿਨ ਸੂਖਿ ਬਿਹਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh tin sookh bihaavai |
lines.english: Those who remember God abide in peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਸੇ ਮੁਖ ਸੁਹਾਵੇ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh se mukh suhaave |
lines.english: Those who remember God - their faces are beautiful.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਤਿਨ ਆਤਮੁ ਜੀਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh tin aatam jeetaa |
lines.english: Those who remember God conquer their souls.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਤਿਨ ਅਨਦ ਘਨੇਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh tin anad ghanere |
lines.english: Those who remember God experience all sorts of joys.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਤਿਨ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਰੀਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh tin niramal reetaa |
lines.english: Those who remember God have a pure and spotless lifestyle.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਬਸਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਨੇਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kau simareh baseh har nere |
lines.english: Those who remember God abide near the Lord.
By the Grace of the Saints, one remains awake and aware, night and day.
O Nanak, this meditative remembrance comes only by perfect destiny. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਬਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kai simaran har gun baanee |
lines.english: Remembering God, one speaks the Glorious Praises of the Lord.
Remembering God, one's works are accomplished.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਝੂਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kai simaran kabahu na jhoore |
lines.english: Remembering God, one never grieves.
Remembering God, the heart-lotus blossoms forth.
Remembering God, one is absorbed into the state of intuitive ease.
Remembering God, one attains the unchanging position.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖੁ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਸਿਮਰਨ ਕਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: sukh prabh simaran kaa ant na paar |
lines.english: The peace of the meditative remembrance of God has no end or limitation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਮਰਹਿ ਸੇ ਜਨ ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਮਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: simareh se jan jin kau prabh meaa |
lines.english: They alone remember Him, upon whom God bestows His Grace.
Remembering God, the unstruck melody vibrates.
Remembering the Lord, His devotees are famous and radiant.
Nanak seeks the Sanctuary of those humble beings. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਨੀਚ ਚਹੁ ਕੁੰਟ ਜਾਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: har simaran neech chahu kuntt jaate |
lines.english: Remembering the Lord, the lowly become known in all four directions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਭਏ ਸਿਧ ਜਤੀ ਦਾਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: har simaran bhe sidh jatee daate |
lines.english: Remembering the Lord, we become Siddhas, celibates and givers.
Remembering the Lord, the Vedas were composed.
For the remembrance of the Lord, the whole world was established.
For the remembrance of the Lord, He created the whole creation.
Remember, remember in meditation the Lord, the Creator, the Cause of causes.
By His Grace, He Himself bestows understanding.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਸਿਮਰਨੁ ਤਿਨਿ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੮॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh har simaran tin paaeaa |8|1|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukh attains the remembrance of the Lord. ||8||1||
In the remembrance of the Lord, He Himself is Formless.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਣਿ ਤੁਮੑਾਰੀ ਆਇਓ ਨਾਨਕ ਕੇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਸਾਥ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: saran tumaaree aaeo naanak ke prabh saath |1|
lines.english: I have come seeking Your Sanctuary. O God, please be with Nanak! ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੀਨ ਦਰਦ ਦੁਖ ਭੰਜਨਾ ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਨਾਥ ਅਨਾਥ ॥
lines.translit: deen darad dukh bhanjanaa ghatt ghatt naath anaath |
lines.english: O Destroyer of the pains and the suffering of the poor, O Master of each and every heart, O Masterless One:
Salok:
